title: salix glauca subsp. acutifolia
intro: Salix glauca subsp. acutifolia, also known as willow or white willow, is a subspecies of the Salix glauca plant, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. The Salix glauca subsp. acutifolia contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. Its medicinal properties have been used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, offering health benefits such as relief from menstrual cramps, arthritis, and headaches. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, with the most common preparation being a tea made from the bark, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
